department: Duncan of Jordanstone College of Art & Design (DJCAD)
admission_requirements: Relevant first degree or equivalent qualification in a relevant subject. Applicants who can demonstrate appropriate professional experience may also apply. US Bachelor Degree: GPA 3.0–3.69/4.0. A 500-word personal statement is required explaining background and motivation, a brief summary of research interest with contemporary art and theory references, relevant knowledge/skills/work experience, and how the course will benefit the applicant.

language_requirements.english: IELTS Academic: Overall 6.0 (Writing 6.0, Listening 5.5, Reading 5.5, Speaking 5.5); AP English Language & Composition / AP English Literature & Composition: Grade 3

portfolio_details: One PDF document with up to 15 pages of work plus one contents page (max 50MB). Each page should contain high quality images with brief descriptions indicating media, size and date produced. Up to 5 minutes of video content optional via public link (YouTube/Vimeo/Dropbox). Portfolio must demonstrate creative ideas, skills, developmental process (sketchbook pages), and professional presentation.
application_process: Apply directly via the University's eVision system. Upload official transcript, degree certificate, evidence of English language proficiency, passport (international applicants), CV, academic reference, professional reference, and personal statement. Portfolio submission required after invitation (within 2 weeks of invitation). Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis; early application recommended.

application_deadlines.notes: Due to high demand, apply as soon as possible. Courses may close early if all vacancies are filled. International students should allow sufficient time for visa processing.

curriculum_summary: Studio-based course with 180 credits across 3 terms. Semester 1: Research and Development in Practice (20 credits) + MFA Studio Research and Practice 1 (40 credits). Semester 2: MFA Studio Research and Practice 2 (40 credits) + one optional module (20 credits). Summer: MFA Realisation of Practice (60 credits).

tuition.notes: Scotland/UK/Ireland students: £11,000/year. International students: £23,700/year. Scholarships available: Global Excellence Scholarship (£8,000) and Vice Chancellor's Americas Scholarship (£4,000). Scholarships cannot be combined.

scholarship_info: Global Excellence Scholarship Postgraduate: £8,000 (requires 1st class degree or equivalent). Vice Chancellor's Americas Postgraduate Scholarship: £4,000. Scholarships cannot be combined. International fee after Global Excellence: £15,700; after Americas Scholarship: £19,700.
highlights: Studio-centred MFA focusing on contemporary art practice and critical thinking
Dedicated adviser supporting personal study evolution throughout the course
Access to DJCAD workshops, studios and exhibition spaces
Annual Master Show showcasing work to industry experts and VIP guests
Strong links with local and international art communities for internships and networking
Interdisciplinary optional modules in Experimental Writing Studio and Making & Materiality
